compiled_state.title: Foundation: PRECONDITIONS event, minimal tier, flat persistence helpers, brainstorm writer, and restart-rate baseline capture
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Scope
Define the PRECONDITIONS event schema at minimal tier, flat-file persistence in the existing ticket directory, reader/writer helpers with LWW resolution, brainstorm stage writer, and the pre-epic workflow-restart-rate baseline collector.

## Done Definitions
- Event shape: append-only `<ts>-<uuid>-PRECONDITIONS.json` in existing ticket dir (no new dirs).
- Minimal-tier schema fields: `schema_version=1`, `manifest_depth="minimal"`, `gate_verdicts[]` composite-keyed by (gate_name, session_id, worktree_id), `evidence_ref{source_type,pointer,rationale}`, `affects_fields[]`.
- Helper `_write_preconditions(ticket_id, stage, depth, payload)` persists atomic-rename.
- Helper `_read_latest_preconditions(ticket_id, stage=None, ...)` resolves composite-key conflicts via timestamp-ordered LWW across the full event set.
- Latest-selection semantics pinned: "latest" = highest timestamp per (stage, session_id, worktree_id); ties broken by event filename lexical ordering; clock-skew tolerance derived from monotonic filename ordering, not wall clock.
- Stage filter: reader accepts optional stage arg; when omitted returns per-stage latest.
- Brainstorm stage writer emits a PRECONDITIONS event at brainstorm:complete.
- Restart-rate baseline collector: sub-task scans git/ticket history to capture pre-epic workflow-restart baseline at Story 1 merge (BEFORE Story 2 validator lands); baseline persisted as versioned fixture consumed by Story 6.
- Unit tests: writer, reader-by-stage, LWW resolution across concurrent events (same composite key, different timestamps), schema validation, baseline collector.
- Concurrency test: two simulated concurrent writers on same composite key produce two events; reader returns latest by timestamp; no writes lost.

## Considerations
- [Reliability] Flat append-only means write-time merge is impossible; all conflict resolution is read-side LWW — document this invariant.
- [Maintainability] Helper signatures are load-bearing for Stories 2, 4, 5, 6 — pin before downstream stories start.
- [Testing] Baseline collector must run against pre-Story-2 state; capture timing is strictly before Story 2 merges.

## Walking Skeleton
Yes — foundation slice of the end-to-end vertical (brainstorm write → preplanning read); Story 2 completes the skeleton.
